steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.difficilius: 'difficilius' is a comparative adjective meaning 'more difficult'

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — not 'esse'
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand) — not 'esse'
hints.sedeo, sedere, sedi, sessum: These are the parts of 'sedere' (to sit) — not 'esse'

hints.Nominative: While 'vita' can be nominative, after 'in' it is ablative (in life)
hints.Vocative: Cicero is not addressing life — 'vita' follows 'in' (ablative of sphere)
hints.Accusative: Accusative after 'in' would mean motion toward — here 'in vita' means 'in life' (static)

hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'vita, vitae' is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — 'vita' with -a is 1st declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'vita' is 1st declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'oratio' — 'oratione' is the ablative singular
hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'orationem' — 'oratione' is ablative after 'in'

hints.1st: 1st declension ablative ends in -a — 'oratio, orationis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension ablative ends in -o — 'oratione' with -e ending is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension ablative ends in -u — 'oratione' is 3rd declension

hints.Positive neuter nominative: The positive would be 'difficile' — the -ius ending signals the comparative
hints.Superlative neuter nominative: The superlative would be 'difficillimum' — '-ius' is comparative
hints.Comparative masculine nominative: Masculine comparative nominative would be 'difficilior' — '-ius' is the neuter form

choices: A purpose clause: in order that

hints.A purpose clause: in order that: 'Ut...sic' here is correlative (just as...so), not a purpose clause
hints.A result clause: so that: 'Ut...sic' is a comparative correlation, not a result construction
hints.A concession: although: 'Ut' can mean 'although,' but paired with 'sic' it means 'just as...so'

translation: For just as in life, so in speech nothing is more difficult.
